name: Pane Di Mattina Alla Siciliana
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------------EGG WASH----------------------------------
1. In a large stainless steel bowl, whisk together whipping cream, egg yolk, and Marsala.
2. Brush olive oil over the surface of the bowl.
3. Dissolve yeast and 1 teaspoon sugar in 1 cup scalded milk. Set aside for 10 minutes.
4. Add 1-1/2 cups flour and remaining milk to the yeast mixture. Mix until a soft, silky dough forms (about 7-8 minutes).
5. Cover and let rise for 5 hours.
6. Soak currents and raisins in Marsala for at least 1 hour.
7. In a small saucepan over low heat, melt butter and shortening together.
8. Add fennel seeds to the mixture and allow it to cool.
9. Add eggs one at a time to the fennel seed mixture, mixing after each addition.
10. Stir in lemon rind and remaining sugar. Set aside.
11. When the dough has risen for 5 hours, add the fennel seed mixture and mix well.
12. Gradually add more flour (1/2 cup at a time) until the dough is firm enough to knead.
13. Turn out onto a lightly floured board and knead until soft and smooth (about 10-15 minutes), adding more flour as needed.
14. During the final 5 minutes, fold in raisins and currents.
15. Shape dough into 2 loaves.
16. Grease two nine-inch pans with remaining shortening.
17. Place dough in pans, cover, and let rise until doubled in bulk (this may take up to 3 hours).
18. Preheat oven to 375°F.
19. Brush the loaves with egg wash 5 minutes before baking.
20. Brush again immediately before baking.
21. Bake until loaves are golden brown and sound hollow when tapped, about 35 minutes.
22. Turn out onto a wire rack and cool.
